classes.name: openstates.data.models.division.Division
documentation:
A political geography, which may have multiple boundaries over its lifetime.

Types of divisions include, among others:
* Governmental jurisdiction - A division that a government has jurisdiction over.
(e.g. North Carolina)
* Political district - A division that elects a representative to a legislature.
(e.g. North Carolina Congressional District 4)
* Service zone - An area to which a government provides a service.
(e.g. Washington DC Police District 105)

classes.name: openstates.data.models.jurisdiction.Jurisdiction
documentation:
A Jurisdiction represents a logical unit of governance.

Examples would include: the United States Federal Government, the Government
of the District of Columbia, the Lexington-Fayette Urban County Government,
or the Wake County Public School System.

classes.name: openstates.data.models.bill.SearchableBill
documentation:
This model associates a single version's text with a given bill.

This is done for a few reasons:
    * bills with multiple versions aren't weighted more heavily than others
    * this makes querying quite a bit more efficient (no need to deduplicate results)

We'll also store error results, assuming that they're somewhat persistent.

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.de.handle_delaware
documentation: the sample.csv includes only MSWord files for some Delaware laws
in 'real life' we'll want to make sure to feed to this scraper the PDF link,
Docxes are ignored, PDFs will be handled IFF 'HCR' is in the title.

modules.name: openstates.data.common
documentation: Module for declaration of common constants available throughout Open Civic Data code.

variables.name: openstates.data.common._keys
documentation:
Policy on addition of new types here:

Because these lists are strictly enforced in lots of code for the purposes of data quality
we have a fairly liberal policy on amendment.

If a type is needed and is not duplicative of another type, it will be accepted.

At the moment, because of this policy, no method exists to extend these lists, instead we will
strive for them to be comprehensive.

The only exception to this would be translations, which should simply exist as translations of
the display name (2nd attribute).

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.utils._text_near_line_numbers
documentation: used for before & after line numbers

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.common.extract_sometimes_numbered_pdf
documentation:
A few states have bills both with numbered lines and without.
In these cases, we need to look at the start of the lines
to determine which extraction function to use.

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.common.extract_pre_tag_html
documentation:
Many states that provide bill text on HTML webpages (e.g. AK, FL)
have the text inside <pre> tags (for preformatted text).

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.common.extract_from_p_tags_html
documentation:
For a few states providing bill text in HTML, we just want to get all
the text in paragraph tags on the page. There may be several paragraphs.

functions.name: openstates.fulltext.common.extract_from_code_tags_html
documentation:
Some states (e.g. IL) have the bill text inside
<code> tags (as it renders as fixed-width).

classes.name: openstates.scrape.base.BaseModel

methods.name: openstates.scrape.base.BaseModel.validate
documentation:
Validate that we have a valid object.

On error, this will raise a `ScrapeValueError`

This also expects that the schemas assume that omitting required
in the schema asserts the field is optional, not required. This is
due to upstream schemas being in JSON Schema v3, and not validictory's
modified syntax.
^ TODO: FIXME

This is the base class for all the Open Civic objects. This contains
common methods and abstractions for OCD objects.

methods.name: openstates.importers.base.BaseImporter.resolve_json_id
documentation:
Given an id found in scraped JSON, return a DB id for the object.

params:
    json_id:        id from json
    allow_no_match: just return None if id can't be resolved

returns:
    database id

raises:
    ValueError if id couldn't be resolved

methods.name: openstates.importers.base.BaseImporter._create_related
documentation:
create DB objects related to a base object
    obj:            a base object to create related
    related:        dict mapping field names to lists of related objects
    subfield_list:  where to get the next layer of subfields

BaseImporter

Override:
    get_object(data)
    limit_spec(spec)                [optional, required if pseudo_ids are used]
    prepare_for_db(data)            [optional]
    postimport()                    [optional]
    update_computed_fields(obj)     [optional]

methods.name: openstates.utils.instrument.Instrumentation.__init__
documentation:
We're currently leveraging https://github.com/civic-eagle/statsd-http-proxy
to have an easy to reason about stats aggregation tool
on the other end of an internet connection

Essentially statsd-style metrics, we can rely on the aggregator
to continually emit these stats so we get useful stats for monitoring/reporting

methods.name: openstates.utils.instrument.Instrumentation._send_stats
documentation:
Needs to be broken out from _process_metric to have a
"write at shutdown" function (force=True)
Even with force=True, we should only write when there is data
to write. Otherwise, just skip things.

methods.name: openstates.utils.instrument.Instrumentation.write_stats
documentation:
Ensure consistent formatting of data objects to add to batch for sending
Primarily, we'll force a timestamp on every metric in a consistent manner

methods.name: openstates.utils.instrument.Instrumentation.close
documentation:
"Shut down" our instrumentation connection
Currently just forces any batched stats out
Keep as a wrapper script for later extensibility
